title: Wire entry/exit/resolution code paths to populate attribution fields; backfill existing trades
objective: Populate attribution fields for every trade by hooking into entry, exit, and resolution code paths, and backfill existing trades where data can be reconstructed.
required_work: 1. Wire entry hook to populate entry_model_prob, entry_market_prob, expected_edge_pct, actual_fill_px, actual_fill_size.
2. Wire exit hook to populate exit_reason, exit_model_prob, exit_market_prob, hold_seconds.
3. Wire resolution_watcher to populate resolved_winner, model_side_was_right, trade_class.
4. Build tools/backfill_attribution.py to reconstruct attribution for pre-existing trades.
5. All writes use core/attribution module; JSONL + DB row both updated.
acceptance_criteria: A new paper trade exercised end-to-end writes non-NULL values for all attribution columns at the appropriate lifecycle point
Resolution correctly populates trade_class per enum rules
Backfill script is idempotent and distinguishes reliable (attribution_version=1) from best-effort (attribution_version=0)
No change in entry/exit decision behavior
verification: Run a synthetic paper trade end-to-end and show all attribution columns populated in DB
Run the backfill once, then again — confirm second run no-op
Show before/after counts of NULL attribution rows
Show one row per trade_class enum value (synthesized is fine)
